2604.09482.title: Process Reward Agents for Steering Knowledge-Intensive Reasoning
2604.09482.paper_summary: Reasoning in knowledge-intensive domains remains challenging as intermediate steps are often not locally verifiable: unlike math or code, evaluating step correctness may require synthesizing clues across large external knowledge sources. As a result, subtle errors can propagate through reasoning traces, potentially never to be detected. Prior work has proposed process reward models (PRMs), including retrieval-augmented variants, but these methods operate post hoc, scoring completed trajectories, which prevents their integration into dynamic inference procedures. Here, we introduce Process Reward Agents (PRA), a test-time method for providing domain-grounded, online, step-wise rewards to a frozen policy. In contrast to prior retrieval-augmented PRMs, PRA enables search-based decoding to rank and prune candidate trajectories at every generation step. Experiments on multiple medical reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that PRA consistently outperforms strong baselines, achieving 80.8% accuracy on MedQA with Qwen3-4B, a new state of the art at the 4B scale. Importantly, PRA generalizes to unseen frozen policy models ranging from 0.5B to 8B parameters, improving their accuracy by up to 25.7% without any policy model updates. More broadly, PRA suggests a paradigm in which frozen reasoners are decoupled from domain-specific reward modules, allowing the deployment of new backbones in complex domains without retraining.
2604.09482.paper_summary_zh: 知識密集型領域的推理仍然具有挑戰性，因為中間步驟往往無法在本地驗證：與數學或程式碼不同，評估步驟的正確性可能需要綜合來自大型外部知識來源的線索。結果，微妙的錯誤可能會在推理過程中傳播，潛在地永遠無法被檢測到。先前的研究提出了過程獎勵模型（PRMs），包括檢索增強變體，但這些方法是在事後運作，對已完成的軌跡進行評分，這阻礙了它們在動態推理程序中的整合。在這裡，我們介紹了過程獎勵代理（PRA），這是一種在測試時提供基於領域的、在線的、逐步獎勵給冷凍策略的方法。與先前的檢索增強PRMs相比，PRA使基於搜索的解碼能夠在每個生成步驟中對候選軌跡進行排名和修剪。在多個醫療推理基準上的實驗表明，PRA始終超越強基線，在MedQA上以Qwen3-4B達到80.8%的準確率，這是4B規模的新最先進技術。重要的是，PRA能夠推廣到未見過的冷凍策略模型，參數範圍從0.5B到8B，將其準確性提高了多達25.7%，而無需任何策略模型更新。更廣泛地說，PRA提出了一種範式，其中冷凍推理器與特定領域的獎勵模塊解耦，允許在複雜領域中部署新的骨幹而無需重新訓練。
2604.09482.author: Jiwoong Sohn et.al.
2604.09482.authors: Jiwoong Sohn, Tomasz Sternal, Kenneth Styppa, Torsten Hoefler, Michael Moor
